arguments.name: msbuild-review
arguments.prompt:
Review all MSBuild project files in this .NET solution for anti-patterns, duplication, modernization opportunities, and best practices violations. The solution is at /tmp/sv-956a3a502714471bab7ec99e9d8eacd0/ContosoApp.sln with source under src/ and tests under test/.

Specifically look for:
1. Duplicated properties across .csproj files that should be centralized in Directory.Build.props/targets
2. Scattered package versions that should use Central Package Management
3. Anti-patterns (hardcoded paths, restated SDK defaults, Exec instead of built-in tasks, etc.)
4. Modernization opportunities (SDK-style format issues, unnecessary explicit includes, etc.)
5. Missing or poorly structured Directory.Build.props/targets
6. Any other MSBuild best practice violations

Produce a detailed report with actionable improvement suggestions, organized by category. For each issue, show the current state (BAD) and the recommended fix (GOOD).

I now have a complete picture of the solution. Here is my detailed MSBuild code review report:

---

# üîç MSBuild Code Review Report ‚Äî ContosoApp Solution

## Solution Overview

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Projects | 5 (3 src + 2 test) |
| SDK Style | ‚úÖ All projects are SDK-style |
| Target Framework | net8.0 (all projects) |
| Directory.Build.props | ‚ùå Missing |
| Directory.Build.targets | ‚ùå Missing |
| Directory.Packages.props | ‚ùå Missing (no Central Package Management) |
| .editorconfig | ‚ùå Missing |

---

## üî¥ Errors (Likely to Cause Bugs or Build Issues)

### 1. Inconsistent Package Versions Across Projects (Version Drift)

The same package has **different versions** in different projects, which can cause subtle runtime failures or assembly binding conflicts.

| Package | ContosoApp.Core | ContosoApp.Services | ContosoApp.Api |
|---------|-----------------|---------------------|----------------|
| `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` | **8.0.0** | **8.0.1** | **8.0.0** |

| Package | Core.Tests | Api.Tests |
|---------|-----------|-----------|
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | **17.10.0** | **17.9.0** |
| `xunit` | **2.8.1** | **2.7.1** |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | **2.8.1** | **2.5.6** |

**Risk**: `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` version mismatch between Core (8.0.0) and Services (8.0.1) can produce **MSB3277** (assembly version conflicts) or unexpected runtime behavior when both assemblies are loaded.

---

## üü° Warnings (Anti-patterns That Should Be Fixed)

### 2. No `Directory.Build.props` ‚Äî Massive Property Duplication

**Every single project** copy-pastes these 7 identical properties:

```xml
<!-- BAD: Repeated in ALL 5 .csproj files -->
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

**GOOD**: Create a `Directory.Build.props` at the solution root:

```xml
<!-- /Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Test projects don't produce NuGet packages -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

Then each `.csproj` can be reduced to just its SDK, packages, and project references. This eliminates **40+ lines of duplicated XML** across the solution.

---

### 3. No Central Package Management (Scattered Versions)

Package versions are scattered across 5 files with no single source of truth.

**BAD** (current state ‚Äî versions in each `.csproj`):
```xml
<!-- In ContosoApp.Core.csproj -->
<PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />

<!-- In ContosoApp.Api.csproj -->
<PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
```

**GOOD**: Create `Directory.Packages.props` at solution root:

```xml
<!-- /Directory.Packages.props -->
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Shared dependencies -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Test infrastructure -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

Then in `.csproj` files, drop the `Version` attribute:
```xml
<!-- GOOD: Version centrally managed -->
<PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
```

---

### 4. Duplicated Custom Target Across 3 Source Projects

The `PrintBuildInfo` target is copy-pasted identically in all 3 source projects:

```xml
<!-- BAD: Duplicated in Core, Api, and Services .csproj files -->
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

**GOOD**: Move to `Directory.Build.targets`:

```xml
<!-- /Directory.Build.targets -->
<Project>
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

This target automatically applies to all projects in the tree. If you only want it for `src/` projects, place it at `src/Directory.Build.targets` (with a `<Import>` of the parent if needed).

---

### 5. Duplicated `DocumentationFile` Property in All Source Projects

```xml
<!-- BAD: Repeated in Core, Api, Services -->
<PropertyGroup>
  <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```

**GOOD**: Centralize into `Directory.Build.props` with a condition so test projects don't generate docs:

```xml
<!-- In Directory.Build.props -->
<PropertyGroup Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Note: In modern SDK-style projects, simply setting `<GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>` automatically outputs to `$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml`. The explicit `<DocumentationFile>` path is **restating the SDK default**.

---

### 6. `LangVersion>latest` Restates SDK Default for .NET 8

For `net8.0`, the SDK already defaults `LangVersion` to the latest stable C# version (C# 12). Setting `latest` goes beyond this to include preview features, which may be unintentional.

```xml
<!-- BAD: Potentially unintentional ‚Äî "latest" includes preview features -->
<LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
```

**GOOD**: Either remove it (to accept the SDK default of C# 12 for net8.0), or be explicit:
```xml
<!-- Option A: Remove entirely ‚Äî SDK defaults to C# 12 for net8.0 -->

<!-- Option B: Be explicit if you want the latest stable -->
<LangVersion>12</LangVersion>
```

---

## üîµ Suggestions (Improvements for Maintainability & Performance)

### 7. Custom Target `PrintBuildInfo` Lacks `Inputs`/`Outputs`

The `PrintBuildInfo` target runs on **every build** regardless of whether anything changed, because it has no `Inputs`/`Outputs` declaration.

```xml
<!-- BAD: Always runs, even on no-op builds -->
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

For a diagnostic `<Message>` target this is minor (it's cheap), but it breaks true no-op builds. If this target grows to include actual work later, it will degrade incremental build performance.

**GOOD** (if you keep it): Accept this is intentional for diagnostics, or gate it:
```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build"
        Condition="'$(PrintBuildDiagnostics)' == 'true'">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

---

### 8. Missing `PrivateAssets="all"` on `xunit.runner.visualstudio`

The `xunit.runner.visualstudio` package is a build/test-time dependency and should not flow to downstream consumers.

```xml
<!-- BAD: May leak to consumers if test project is referenced -->
<PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
```

**GOOD**:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1"
                  PrivateAssets="all" />
```

(Same for `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`.)

---

### 9. Multiple `<PropertyGroup>` Blocks Without Conditions

In the source projects (Core, Api, Services), there are **two** `<PropertyGroup>` blocks with no `Condition` attribute ‚Äî one for general properties and one for `DocumentationFile`. These should be merged into a single `<PropertyGroup>`:

```xml
<!-- BAD: Fragmented without reason -->
<PropertyGroup>
  <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  ...
</PropertyGroup>
<PropertyGroup>
  <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```

**GOOD**: Single unconditional `<PropertyGroup>`:
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  ...
  <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```

(Moot point once centralized ‚Äî included for completeness.)

---

### 10. Consider Adding a `.editorconfig` and `global.json`

The solution has `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` enabled but no `.editorconfig` to define the code style rules. Without it, the property has limited effect.

Additionally, a `global.json` pinning the SDK version ensures reproducible builds across developer machines and CI.

---

## üü¢ Positive (Things Done Well)

| Practice | Status |
|----------|--------|
| All projects use SDK-style format | ‚úÖ |
| `Nullable` enabled everywhere | ‚úÖ |
| `ImplicitUsings` enabled | ‚úÖ |
| `TreatWarningsAsErrors` enforced | ‚úÖ |
| Test projects are marked `IsPackable=false` | ‚úÖ |
| Project references use relative paths (not absolute) | ‚úÖ |
| No `packages.config` (all use PackageReference) | ‚úÖ |
| No legacy `<Import>` of MSBuild tools path | ‚úÖ |
| Single `TargetFramework` per project (no unnecessary multi-targeting) | ‚úÖ |

---

## üìã Recommended Final Project Structure

After applying all recommendations:

```
ContosoApp/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Directory.Build.props          ‚Üê Shared properties
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Directory.Build.targets        ‚Üê Shared targets (PrintBuildInfo)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Directory.Packages.props       ‚Üê Central Package Management
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ global.json                    ‚Üê SDK version pin
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .editorconfig                  ‚Üê Code style rules
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ContosoApp.sln
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ src/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ContosoApp.Core/
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ ContosoApp.Core.csproj          ‚Üê ~5 lines
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ContosoApp.Api/
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ ContosoApp.Api.csproj           ‚Üê ~10 lines
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ ContosoApp.Services/
‚îÇ       ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ ContosoApp.Services.csproj      ‚Üê ~8 lines
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ test/
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ContosoApp.Core.Tests/
    ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ ContosoApp.Core.Tests.csproj    ‚Üê ~8 lines
    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ ContosoApp.Api.Tests/
        ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ ContosoApp.Api.Tests.csproj     ‚Üê ~8 lines
```

### Example Minimal `ContosoApp.Core.csproj` After Cleanup:

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />

(Down from 27 lines to 7 lines!)
